docs.title: Obsidian Sync Server
docs.text:
A headless sync hub running as a Docker container that integrates:

  • 💎 Obsidian Sync (official obsidian-headless client)
  • ☁️ Nextcloud Sync (nextcloudcmd client)
  • 📝 Google Keep (pulling notes via gkeepapi)

docs.title: Concept
docs.text:
The server runs in daemon mode, syncing multiple "sets" periodically. Obsidian Sync runs continuously in the background, keeping local files up to date.

Periodically (every 5 minutes by default), the server will:

  1. Pull Google Keep notes matching your tags and write them as .md files.
  2. Synchronize the folder with a remote Nextcloud directory.

Conflict Resolution

Obsidian is considered authoritative. Nextcloud conflict files are automatically deleted to maintain Obsidian's state.

docs.title: Google Keep
docs.text: To securely authenticate with Google Keep via gkeepapi, standard passwords are not recommended as they may trigger Google's automated security blocks. Instead, use an App Password (recommended) or a Master Token.

docs.title: Using an App Password (Recommended)
docs.text:
An App Password is a 16-digit passcode that gives an app or device permission to access your Google Account. This requires 2-Step Verification to be enabled.

  1. Go to your Google Account.
  2. Navigate to Security on the left panel.
  3. Ensure 2-Step Verification is turned On.
  4. Search for "App passwords" in the top search bar (or find it under the 2-Step Verification settings).
  5. Enter a name for the app (e.g., "Obsidian Syncer") and click Create.
  6. Copy the generated 16-character password (you can omit the spaces) and use it as your KEEP_PASSWORD environment variable.

docs.title: Using a Master Token (Advanced)
docs.text:
If you prefer not to use an App Password, you can extract a Master Token. The Master Token is a long string that acts as a secure session token.

Use the Master Token directly as the KEEP_PASSWORD in your configuration. Note that Master Tokens may eventually expire or be revoked by Google if suspicious activity is detected.

Nextcloud Credentials

Pass your Nextcloud credentials via environment variables (NC_USER and NC_PASSWORD). The configuration file will dynamically expand these variables to prevent hardcoding secrets.

docs.title: Obsidian Headless
docs.text:
The obsidian-headless tool requires an active Obsidian Sync subscription. To authenticate, you should run the container interactively once:

  1. Open a terminal in the container: docker exec -it obsidian-syncer bash
  2. Run ob login and follow the prompts.
  3. Link the vault with ob sync-setup --vault "YourRemoteVaultName" --path /vaults/my_vault.
  4. Restart the container so the Python daemon picks up the new sync process.

docs.title: Setup Configuration
docs.text:
Create a config.yaml file mapping your sets:

config.yaml
sync_sets:
  - name: "my_vault"
    local_dir: "/vaults/my_vault"
    obsidian:
      enabled: true
    nextcloud:
      enabled: true
      url: "https://nextcloud.example.com"
      user: "${NC_USER}"
      password: "${NC_PASSWORD}"
      remote_folder: "/Obsidian/my_vault"
    keep:
      enabled: true
      username: "${KEEP_USER}"
      password: "${KEEP_PASSWORD}" # Use an App Password or Master Token
      tags: ["Gemini"]
      target_folder: "/vaults/my_vault/KeepNotes"

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
Choose your preferred method to run the sync server:

Docker ComposeDocker CLI

For a more persistent setup, use Docker Compose. Create a docker-compose.yml:

docker-compose.yml
services:
  obsidian-syncer:
    image: ghcr.io/janlo/obsidian-syncer:latest
    container_name: obsidian-syncer
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./config.yaml:/config.yaml:ro
      - ./vaults:/vaults
      - ./ob-config:/root/.config
    environment:
      - NC_USER=${NC_USER}
      - NC_PASSWORD=${NC_PASSWORD}
      - KEEP_USER=${KEEP_USER}
      - KEEP_PASSWORD=${KEEP_PASSWORD}

Run it in the background: 

docker compose up -d

Run the container using a bind mount for the configuration, vaults, and obsidian configuration directory:

docker run -d \
  --name obsidian-syncer \
  -v $(pwd)/config.yaml:/config.yaml \
  -v $(pwd)/vaults:/vaults \
  -v $(pwd)/ob-config:/root/.config \
  -e NC_USER=... \
  -e NC_PASSWORD=... \
  -e KEEP_USER=... \
  -e KEEP_PASSWORD=... \
  ghcr.io/janlo/obsidian-syncer:latest
